methods.description: This method involves analyzing historical documents to reconstruct past climate variability. It uses descriptive documentary data such as weather observations, chronicles, and travel diaries to provide information about past climate conditions.

steps.description: Collect historical documents including weather observations, chronicles, daily weather reports, travel diaries, and ship logbooks.
Analyze the documents to extract climate-related information, focusing on precipitation patterns and temperature estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: extract climate data from historical documents

steps.description: Validate the historical data by comparing it with other proxy data and checking the historical validity of the documents.

methods.description: This method involves analyzing early instrumental meteorological data from the late 18th century to describe brief and extreme climate periods.

steps.description: Collect early instrumental records from Sevilla, Cádiz, Málaga, and Granada for the period 1780-1850.
Analyze the instrumental data to describe brief and extreme climate periods, such as floods or unusual temperature events.

methods.description: This method uses regional climate models to simulate past climate conditions, providing a physically consistent climate representation to test reconstructions.

steps.description: Perform climate simulations using the mesoscale model MM5 driven by the global model ECHO-G.
steps.searchable_summary: run climate simulations with MM5 and ECHO-G

steps.description: Calculate 11-year moving averages of the simulation results to obtain a clearer overview of the climate trends.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate moving averages of climate simulation data

steps.description: Use the simulated climate data to test aspects of climate reconstructions, such as variability and physical relationships.
steps.searchable_summary: validate climate reconstructions with simulation data
